mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over consecutive 12-month windows, or positive 20-day returns do not exceed 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within the following trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over the subsequent 60 trading days with p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable consumer prices confirms healthy output growth without inflationary pressure, supporting positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, resulting in negative forward returns despite rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity that supports near-term equity price appreciation over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS drops below zero over any rolling 24-month period, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with payroll changes.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 3.5% triggers financial condition tightening that suppresses risk appetite and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains above 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling real GDP growth indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and drive negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain variance in forward returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in real personal consumption expenditures during periods of high unemployment signal severe demand destruction that forces immediate equity market corrections within one week.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS declines occur alongside falling UNRATE, producing positive 5-day returns in over 65% of cases.
